$comment: Caption + hashtag config for caption-library skill. Pillar-keyed pools. Editable by anyone on the team — skills re-read on every run. Update tone_notes / examples freely as voice evolves.

voice.voice_overs_in_video: FORBIDDEN — silent footage + on-screen text only
voice.$comment_signoff: Account handle is in the design, not the caption. Set default_signoff_in_caption: true if Vhinz wants it appended to every IG caption.

approved_ctas: The mat is open. Come see what we're building.
Words won't show you what we've built. The mat will.
Come feel what belonging looks like on the mat.
Your first class is on us. Come meet the team.
We don't sell Jiu-Jitsu. We invite you to be part of something.
This community has a spot for you. Come find yours.
42 members built this together. Come be part of what's next.

community_daily.tone_notes: Settled, present, warm. Acknowledge the day or moment without overstating it. 1–2 short lines. The image leads.
examples: Start of the week. See you on the mats.
Monday. The mat doesn't care what kind of week you had.
We train hard, but we enjoy the process.
The work between the highlights.
Friday on the mats. Tired. Smiling. Back tomorrow.

member_spotlight.tone_notes: First-person if a quote is available, third-person if not. Honest, specific. Name the journey, not the technique. Avoid 'success story' framing.
examples: Started with zero experience. Still here three months later.
Why I started Jiu-Jitsu. Why I stayed.
Three stripes. Three months. One reason: the people.
Came for fitness. Stayed for the team.

value_trust.tone_notes: Educational, calm, accessible. No jargon walls. The coach speaks (silent clip + text). Lead with what someone learns, not what we offer.
examples: Why fundamentals matter.
What every white belt should know on day one.
The first three things you'll learn.
Posture beats power.

kids_family.tone_notes: PARENT-FACING. The reader is a mom or dad watching from the sideline. Lead with what kids gain — confidence, listening, focus. Never sound like adult-class recruiting.
examples: Confidence. Listening. A safe place to learn.
What 45 minutes on the mat builds in a kid.
Parents — this is what they look like after class.
Discipline starts here.

social_proof.tone_notes: Quietly confident. Let the image do the talking. Never use scarcity language. Numbers are okay if real (member counts, years, etc.).
examples: Weekend training with the team.
Saturday open mat. Show up. Roll. Stay for coffee.
42 of us, building something.
Built together.

leader_voice.tone_notes: Reflective, forward-looking. The voice of the academy, not a pitch. Use 'we' often. Silent clip + text overlay only.
examples: What we're building this week.
The reason we keep showing up.
Built together — one mat, one team.
This is the work behind the highlights.

hashtag_pool.$comment: 12–15 hashtags per post. Always include all brand_anchors. Then add 8–11 from the rotating pool, weighted toward the slot's pillar. Avoid posting the exact same set two weeks in a row — IG flags repetition.

rotation_strategy.$comment: Per ISO week, the caption-library skill picks: (4 brand anchors) + (5 from bjj_general rotated by week-modulo) + (3 from local rotated by week-modulo) + (3 from by_pillar[slot.pillar] rotated by week-modulo). Total = 15. Modulo formula in skill spec.
